description:
A penetration tester has just finished running an automated vulnerability scan.  
    The raw JSON output produced by the scanner has already been placed for you in  

        /home/user/data/scan_results.json  

    A JSON-Schema that formally describes the expected structure of that output is in  

        /home/user/data/scan_schema.json  

    Goal  
    ----  
    1. Using **only a single terminal command** (piped, compound, or semicolon-separated is fine, but launched in one line), do all of the following:  
       a. Verify that every element in /home/user/data/scan_results.json contains **all** of the required keys described by the schema file (`id`, `name`, `severity`, `cvss`).  
          •  If any element is missing a required key, the command must exit with a non-zero status code.  
       b. Generate a concise summary, saved to  

              /home/user/output/critical_summary.json  

          in exactly the format shown below.  The summary must be pretty-printed with 2-space indentation and must end with a single trailing newline.  
          Format the file as:  
          {
            "total_findings": <integer>,
            "critical_findings": <integer>,
            "critical_ids": [
              "<STRING_ID_1>",
              "<STRING_ID_2>",
              ...
            ]
          }  
          • `total_findings`  – total number of objects in the original scan_results.json  
          • `critical_findings` – number of objects whose `severity` value is the string `"critical"`  
          • `critical_ids` – a JSON array containing **only** the `id` values of critical findings, ordered lexicographically ascending.  
       c. The command must **not** write anything to stderr on success.  
       d. On success, print the exact string `SUMMARY_WRITTEN` (without quotes) to stdout.  

    2. The directory /home/user/output already exists and is writable by the current user.  
       Do not create additional directories; simply place the file inside /home/user/output.  

    3. No other files should be modified or created.  

    Automated tests will perform three checks:  
       • The exit code of the command is 0.  
       • /home/user/output/critical_summary.json exists and matches the required JSON byte-for-byte (including indentation and newline).  
       • The last line of stdout is exactly:  
         SUMMARY_WRITTEN  

    Hint: `jq` is installed in the environment.  You may combine multiple jq filters into a single pipeline or use shell logic operators, but everything must run from one invocation line typed into the terminal.
truth:
Initial file system state
    -------------------------
    1. /home/user/data/scan_results.json  
       Contents (with trailing newline):  
       [
         {"id":"VULN-001","name":"Remote Code Execution","severity":"critical","cvss":9.8},
         {"id":"VULN-002","name":"SQL Injection","severity":"high","cvss":9.0},
         {"id":"VULN-003","name":"Directory Traversal","severity":"medium","cvss":6.5},
         {"id":"VULN-004","name":"Buffer Overflow","severity":"critical","cvss":9.1},
         {"id":"VULN-005","name":"Cross-Site Scripting","severity":"low","cvss":4.3}
       ]

    2. /home/user/data/scan_schema.json  
       Contents (with trailing newline):  
       {
         "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
         "type": "array",
         "items": {
           "type": "object",
           "required": ["id","name","severity","cvss"],
           "properties": {
             "id": {"type":"string"},
             "name": {"type":"string"},
             "severity": {"type":"string","enum":["critical","high","medium","low"]},
             "cvss": {"type":"number"}
           },
           "additionalProperties": false
         }
       }

    Directories
    -----------
    • /home/user/output       (exists; permissions 755; owned by user)

    Expected artefact after successful task
    ---------------------------------------
    File: /home/user/output/critical_summary.json  
    Exact contents (note the 2-space indentation and single trailing newline):  
    {
      "total_findings": 5,
      "critical_findings": 2,
      "critical_ids": [
        "VULN-001",
        "VULN-004"
      ]
    }

    Stdout requirement
    ------------------
    The final line of stdout must be exactly:
    SUMMARY_WRITTEN

    No stderr output is expected on success.

    Exit status 0 is required.

    Any deviation (incorrect JSON, wrong ordering, extra whitespace other than the specified indentation/newline, additional files, or stderr output) will cause automated verification to fail.
